1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم إذا السماء انفطرت

1.english: And when the stars fall, scattering,
And when the seas are erupted
And when the [contents of] graves are scattered,
A soul will [then] know what it has put forth and kept back.

1.arabic: يا أيها الإنسان ما غرك بربك الكريم
1.english: O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous,
1.transliteration: Yaaa ayyuhal insaaanu maa gharraka bi Rabbikal kareem

1.english: Who created you, proportioned you, and balanced you?

1.arabic: في أي صورة ما شاء ركبك
1.english: In whatever form He willed has He assembled you.
1.transliteration: Feee ayye sooratim maa shaaa'a rakkabak

1.english: No! But you deny the Recompense.
And indeed, [appointed] over you are keepers,
Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure,
And indeed, the wicked will be in Hellfire.
They will [enter to] burn therein on the Day of Recompense,
And never therefrom will they be absent.
And what can make you know what is the Day of Recompense?
1.transliteration: Wa maaa adraaka maa Yawmud Deen

1.arabic: ثم ما أدراك ما يوم الدين
1.english: Then, what can make you know what is the Day of Recompense?
1.transliteration: Summa maaa adraaka maa Yawmud Deen

1.arabic: يوم لا تملك نفس لنفس شيئا ۖ والأمر يومئذ لله
1.english: It is the Day when a soul will not possess for another soul [power to do] a thing; and the command, that Day, is [entirely] with Allah.
1.transliteration: Yawma laa tamliku nafsul linafsin shai'anw walamru yawma'izil lillaah
